description: This figure characterizes the correlation between Al content, dislocation slip behavior, microstructure, and tensile mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-C-Al low-density steel. Subfigure (a) is a line plot showing W_R, a parameter related to dislocation slip mode, decreases linearly from ~29×10⁻⁴ m to ~10×10⁻⁴ m as Al content rises from 0 to 6 wt.%, indicating a slip mode transition. Subfigure (a1) is a TEM micrograph (200 nm scale bar) of 3 wt.% Al alloy at true strain 0.1, showing dislocation cells (DCs) corresponding to wavy slip, while subfigure (a2) (TEM, 200 nm scale bar, 6 wt.% Al alloy, true strain 0.1) exhibits clear planar slip features. Subfigure (b) is an ECCI micrograph (1 μm scale bar) showing microband structure at true strain ε=0.14. Subfigure (c) displays true stress-strain curve (solid line, tested until fracture) and strain hardening rate curve (dotted line), where the hardening rate first drops rapidly then remains at a high plateau before gradual reduction. The figure supports that increasing Al addition promotes planar dislocation slip, and short range order induced microband formation contributes to the superior work hardening capacity of the low-density steel.
